spec.description: Per-service deep-dive for the Prerender Manager ECS service (`boxel-prerender-manager-${env}`). The manager is a stateless HTTP proxy that fans render requests out to an externally-managed pool of `boxel-prerender-server-${env}` tasks via heartbeat-driven affinity scheduling. Panels cover (1) fleet sizing + resource utilisation from CloudWatch, (2) ALB request rate / latency / host health, (3) manager process lifecycle and pool registry churn (heartbeats, prunes, sweeps) from Loki, (4) proxy throughput by route and backpressure (503 / LRU evictions), (5) upstream + release-batch errors, and (6) per-target-server attribution of proxy outcomes. There is no DB-backed signal — the manager doesn't write to Postgres.

panels.description: ECS task count for `boxel-prerender-manager-${env}` — `Run` is RunningTaskCount, `Need` is DesiredTaskCount (both from ECS/ContainerInsights, Maximum over the panel refresh window). Run < Need means tasks are crashing or the service is under-scaled. Locally this shows 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: Tasks (Run / Need)

panels.description: Approximate prerender-server pool size — distinct target URLs that sent a heartbeat in the last minute, derived from the manager's `received heartbeat from ${url}` log line (manager-app.ts:249). Servers heartbeat every ~5s by default; if this drops to 0 the pool is empty and the next request will 503. Compare against the Prerender Server dashboard's task count.
panels.title: Servers in pool

panels.description: 503 responses returned when no prerender-server was usable — either the registry was empty or every registered server was over capacity for the requested affinity (manager-app.ts:1039, 1055). Non-zero usually means the pool is too small for current load (scale prerender-server) or every server is unhealthy.
panels.title: 503 no-servers (window)

panels.description: Times the manager hit its `forceExitTimeoutMs` during shutdown without finishing graceful drain (manager-server.ts:78). Each one is an unclean exit — likely cause is in-flight render requests outliving the drain window. Steady-state should be 0.
panels.title: Force exits (window)

panels.description: ECS CPU and memory utilisation for `boxel-prerender-manager-${env}` on cluster `${env}` — averages over the panel refresh window. The manager is a stateless proxy so CPU should be modest; sustained 90%+ usually means the task is undersized for current request volume. Locally these show 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: CPU & memory utilisation

panels.description: Running vs desired ECS task count over time for `boxel-prerender-manager-${env}`. Run < Need means tasks are crash-looping; Run dropping to 0 is a deploy or outage. Multiple managers normally run behind the ALB for HA — they don't coordinate state, so a single drop is usually safe.
panels.title: Task count over time

panels.description: ALB request rate broken out by HTTP status class — `2xx` and `4xx` from the manager process (HTTPCode_Target_*), `5xx target` from the manager process (timeouts, crashes), `5xx ALB` from the ALB itself (no target available, target unresponsive). Auto-discovered ALB via the `prerender_manager_lb` template variable. Locally shows 'No data' — CloudWatch is staging/production only.
panels.title: ALB request rate by status

panels.description: ALB-measured response time (manager + upstream prerender-server combined) at p50 / p95 / p99. Renders are long-tailed by nature — p99 spiking while p50 stays flat means a few stuck renders are tying up tabs without affecting the median.
panels.title: ALB target response time (p50 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: ALB target-group host health. `Healthy` is what the ALB will route to; `Unhealthy` is tasks failing the health check (GET /). Healthy=0 means clients get 503s. Auto-discovered target group via the `prerender_manager_tg` template variable.
panels.title: ALB hosts (healthy / unhealthy)

panels.description: Manager-process lifecycle events from Loki. `boot` is `prerender manager HTTP listening on port` (manager-server.ts:51) — emitted after Koa binds successfully. `SIGTERM` / `SIGINT` is `Received ${signal}; marking prerender manager as draining` (manager-server.ts:58). `closed` is `Prerender manager listener closed; exiting process` (manager-server.ts:73). `force-exit` is `Forcing prerender manager shutdown after ${ms} ms timeout` (manager-server.ts:78). A `boot` not preceded by a `closed` or `force-exit` in the same task usually means the container died without graceful signal delivery (cgroup OOM, host failure, ECS SIGKILL).

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="prerender-manager"} |~ "Received SIGTERM; marking prerender manager as draining" [1m]))
sum(count_over_time({service="prerender-manager"} |~ "Received SIGINT; marking prerender manager as draining" [1m]))

panels.title: Manager lifecycle stamps

panels.description: Pool-registry churn. `heartbeats` is the rate of `received heartbeat from ${url}` accepted by the manager (manager-app.ts:249) — expect ~12/min per registered server at the default 5s cadence. `sweep-stale` is the sweep loop detecting a missed heartbeat past `PRERENDER_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS` and queuing a prune (manager-app.ts:873). `upstream-prune` is `Pruned prerender server ${url} due to upstream failure` — fetch couldn't reach the server (manager-app.ts:1149). `5xx-prune` is `Pruned prerender server ${url} due to status ${5xx}` — server returned a 5xx and was evicted. A spike in either prune flavour with no corresponding spike in heartbeats is a pool member leaving the registry.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="prerender-manager"} |~ "Pruned prerender server .* due to upstream failure" [1m]))

panels.title: Pool registry events

panels.description: Proxy request volume broken out by endpoint, from `proxying ${label} prerender request` log lines (manager-app.ts:1071). Lets you see whether the manager is mostly handling `visit` (per-card prerender), `module` (compile-time module prerender), `command` (LLM tool-call execution), or `screenshot` traffic — useful when correlating CPU / latency spikes with workload mix.
panels.title: Proxy requests by route

panels.description: Pool saturation signals. `registry-empty` and `no-usable-target` are the two 503 paths (manager-app.ts:1039, 1055) — clients see HTTP 503. `LRU eviction` is `Pressure-mode: evicted affinity` (manager-app.ts:809) — the pool is full and the manager kicked the globally least-recently-used affinity to free a slot. `fallback eviction` is `Fallback eviction: evicted affinity` (manager-app.ts:848) — per-server LRU eviction, a softer signal. Sustained evictions usually mean `PRERENDER_MULTIPLEX` or the prerender-server fleet size is too small for the working set.
panels.title: Backpressure & evictions

panels.description: Upstream-side proxy failures. `upstream-error` is `Upstream error requestId=` — the fetch to the prerender-server failed at the network layer (manager-app.ts:1122). `proxy-exception` is `Error in /<path> proxy requestId=` — an unhandled exception in the proxy route handler (manager-app.ts:1222). `heartbeat-sweep-error` is internal sweep failure (manager-app.ts:890). `release-batch-broadcast` is the outer release-batch handler failure (manager-app.ts:1348). All four are unexpected — non-zero rates mean something broke.
panels.title: Upstream & proxy errors

panels.description: Release-batch fanout outcomes plus client aborts. After a batch finishes, the manager broadcasts `release-batch` to every server assigned to that affinity (manager-app.ts:1295). Per-target failures are split out: `rb-non-2xx` is a server returning a non-ok status, `rb-timeout` is the proxy timeout firing, `rb-network` is a transport failure. `client-aborted` is the upstream client disconnecting mid-render (manager-app.ts:941) — usually a tab closing during a long render, not a manager bug; a sustained rate may indicate ALB-side timeout misalignment.
panels.title: Release-batch failures + client aborts

panels.description: Per-prerender-server-target attribution over the dashboard window. `proxied` is total `proxying ... to ${target}` invocations against that server (manager-app.ts:1071). `proxied_5xx` is `proxied ${label} ... target=${target} status=5..` — completed proxies that returned a server-side error (manager-app.ts:1216). `prunes` is `Pruned prerender server ${target}` regardless of cause (manager-app.ts:1149). A target with high `prunes` is unhealthy and being repeatedly evicted; high `proxied_5xx` without prunes is a server returning errors that aren't quite bad enough to trigger eviction. Click the URL to drill into Logs filtered to that target.

targets.expr: topk(50, sum by (target) (count_over_time({service="prerender-manager"} |~ "proxying .* prerender request" | regexp "to (?P<target>https?://[^ ]+)" [$__range])))
sum by (target) (count_over_time({service="prerender-manager"} |~ "Pruned prerender server" | regexp "Pruned prerender server (?P<target>https?://[^ ]+)" [$__range]))

panels.title: Per-target proxy outcomes (window)

panels.description: Filtered error + lifecycle stream — 503s, prunes, upstream errors, proxy exceptions, release-batch failures, evictions, signal handlers, force-exits, boots, and client aborts. The errors-only view triages 'something is wrong' without scrolling the firehose.

targets.expr: {service="prerender-manager"} |~ "503 No servers|Pruned prerender server|Upstream error requestId=|Error in /.* proxy requestId=|release-batch on .* (returned|timed out|network error)|Pressure-mode: evicted|Fallback eviction: evicted|Received SIG(TERM|INT); marking prerender manager as draining|Forcing prerender manager shutdown|prerender manager HTTP listening on port|Prerender manager listener closed; exiting process|client-aborted after|Heartbeat sweep error|Unhandled error in /release-batch broadcast"

panels.title: Errors & lifecycle stream

panels.description: Last 1000 log lines from prerender-manager tasks (unfiltered). Drill-throughs from Overview land here.
panels.title: Prerender Manager logs (all)

spec.title: Prerender Manager
